operator_scope.semantic_signature: scatter-add with indexed accumulation and possible duplicate target indices

valid_when: Task semantics are scatter-add or embedding/backward-like accumulation where multiple source elements may update the same destination element.
invalid_when: Task semantics are plain scatter overwrite with unique indices, or the OpSpec states duplicate indices cannot occur.
preconditions: Read index tensor dtype, dimension, bounds, and duplicate-index policy from OpSpec.

mechanism: Scatter-add is an accumulation, not a plain store. Duplicate indices must contribute all source values, so a low-level implementation needs atomics, a segmented reduction, or another conflict-resolution strategy consistent with task determinism requirements.

instructions: Validate that index tensors have the declared integer dtype and dimensionality, and guard index values so every read/write target is in bounds.
When duplicate destination indices may appear, use atomic add or an equivalent staged reduction; never replace scatter-add with an unordered plain store.
Do not assume PyTorch-style broadcasting between index and src for scatter_add; implement the shape constraints declared by the task.
If deterministic output is required, use a deterministic accumulation plan or mark the atomic plan invalid for that OpSpec.
Test empty index, duplicate index, boundary index 0, and maximum valid index cases.
action.patch_hint: Separate source iteration from destination address calculation; use atomic add only at the final destination update or pre-aggregate duplicates within a block when the task permits.

expected_effect.correctness: Preserves additive semantics under duplicate scatter targets and avoids out-of-bounds index bugs.
expected_effect.performance: No stable H20 performance claim; atomics may be correct but slow under high collision rates.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Prevents repeated incorrect attempts that use plain stores for scatter-add.
expected_effect.risk: Floating-point atomic accumulation order can be nondeterministic and tolerance-sensitive.

correctness_matrix: high collision and low collision patterns
fp32 reference tolerance for floating accumulation
validation.promotion_policy: Stable semantic/correctness rule; promote performance policy only after H20 collision-distribution benchmarks.

known_failures: Plain scatter store loses contributions when indices collide.
Out-of-range index handling can produce undefined memory access in custom kernels.
Atomic floating-point order can differ from a deterministic CPU/reference order.
boundary_tests: two or more src elements target same output
empty index returns unchanged or empty output as declared

replace_on_new_backend: available atomic add dtypes and deterministic reduction strategy
